pi_items.verified_value: Aerobridge is an open-source Django-based management server for secure drone operations and fleet management
pi_items.source: Web search result: GitHub repository https://github.com/openskies-sh/aerobridge confirms Aerobridge is a real open-source system for drone fleet management. Environment description in env.json also confirms: 'Aerobridge open-source drone/UAV operations management system environment. Aerobridge is a Django-based server for managing drone fleets, flight plans, personnel, and regulatory compliance.'

pi_items.metadata_value: 100-Hour Scheduled Inspection is a standard maintenance activity type
pi_items.verified_value: 100-Hour inspection is a real FAA-mandated maintenance requirement for aircraft under commercial operations or for hire
pi_items.source: Web search results from multiple authoritative sources: 14 CFR § 91.409 (https://www.law.cornell.edu/cfr/text/14/91.409), AOPA aircraft inspection guide, and FAA regulations confirm 100-hour inspections are required for aircraft used in commercial operations. The inspection must be performed every 100 hours of flight time.

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on Aerobridge, a real open-source Django-based drone fleet management system. The task requires logging a 100-Hour Scheduled Inspection activity, which is a real FAA-mandated maintenance inspection for aircraft under commercial operations. The metadata specifies three critical file paths: maintenance_task_info.txt (randomly populated with a target aircraft identifier from the Aerobridge database), activity_confirmation.txt (to be created as proof of completion), and the expected activity name pattern '100-Hour Scheduled Inspection'. All metadata values are verified to be consistent across setup, task description, verification logic, and export scripts. The aircraft selection is synthetic (randomly selected from database records at runtime), but the maintenance activity type references actual aviation regulatory requirements.
